description: The Toufan Organization (Organization of Toilers of Iran, Sazman-e Toufan) is Iran's principal Maoist political organization, distinct from the pro-Soviet [Tudeh Party of Iran](/orgs/tudeh-party-of-iran) and from the guerrilla-oriented Fadaiyan currents. Founded in 1964 as a Maoist breakaway from the Tudeh Party, Toufan embraced the Sino-Soviet split and aligned with the People's Republic of China against Soviet revisionism. The organization participated in the pre-revolutionary opposition and remained active after the [1979 revolution](/events/iranian-revolution-1979), publishing theoretical journals from exile. Toufan has maintained a consistent Maoist ideological line and criticised both the Soviet-aligned left and the more popular worker-communist tendency associated with [Mansoor Hekmat](/people/mansoor-hekmat).
editorial_notes: Maoist. Founded 1964 as Tudeh splinter. Distinct from pro-Soviet left. Active in exile publishing.
